meta.note_ltc: LTC benchmarks use bench_bitcoin native harness (IQR-trimmed median). min-time=3000ms.
meta.note_doge: DOGE benchmarks use older CSV harness (min/max/average over many iterations). No sign-specific bench in Dogecoin suite.
meta.ltc_backend_ultrafast: UltrafastSecp256k1 v4.0.0 via cmake/secp256k1.cmake SECP256K1_BACKEND=ultrafast, SECP256K1_CORE_BACKEND_MODE=ON
meta.doge_backend_bundled: bundled secp256k1 subtree compiled via autotools

SignSchnorrWithMerkleRoot.note: Schnorr sign with full tapscript merkle root. ~18% faster.

SignSchnorrWithNullMerkleRoot.note: Schnorr sign without merkle root (keypath spend). ~19% faster.

SignTransactionSchnorr.note: Full Schnorr tx sign with key tweaking. Within noise (overlapping ranges). C++11→C++20 shim overhead.

VerifyScriptP2TR_KeyPath.note: P2TR keypath verification. Slight regression (~5%). Verify uses variable-time wNAF in both — overhead from larger .text competing for I-cache.

VerifyScriptP2WPKH.note: P2WPKH verify. Regression ~13%. Larger .text / I-cache pressure without LTO. LTO expected to close this gap.

BIP324_ECDH.note: BIP-324 X-only ECDH. Statistically identical (within noise).

AssembleBlock.note: Block assembly (no secp hotpath). Statistically identical.

dogecoin.unit: microseconds (average per operation from CSV harness)
dogecoin.source: bench/bench_dogecoin (full run, no filter — no sign-specific bench in suite)
dogecoin.note: Dogecoin benchmark suite does not include Sign or ECDSA benchmarks. Comparison covers hashing, bloom, mempool, script utils.

build_issues_notes: LTC: cmake/secp256k1.cmake missing no-op add_secp256k1() stub for ultrafast branch — fixed inline.
LTC: Cap'n Proto missing — resolved with -DENABLE_IPC=OFF.
DOGE: boost_filesystem/.so not installed system-wide — resolved by downloading debs without root and extracting to /tmp/boost_pkgs/extracted.
DOGE: Makefile.am hardcoded LIBSECP256K1=secp256k1/libsecp256k1.la — patched with USE_ULTRAFAST_SECP256K1 conditional.
DOGE: contrib/ultrafast-secp256k1/build.sh copies only thin shim .a (1 object) but fastsecp256k1.a contains all 61 objects — created libsecp256k1_combined.a merging both archives.
DOGE: boost 1.63 in depends/ fails to build with GCC 14 (implicit function declarations treated as errors) — skipped depends, used extracted system debs instead.
No sign-specific benchmark exists in Dogecoin's bench suite — secp256k1 is only exercised indirectly via key operations in CCheckQueueSpeed.
